name: Raspberry and Hazelnut Shortbread Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted butter (cold and cut into small cubes)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch, 11/27/95
Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Spread hazelnuts on a baking sheet; bake until skins crack and nuts are lightly toasted, approximately 10 to 15 minutes.
Transfer nuts to a large dish towel; rub vigorously to remove loosened skins. Separate nuts from skins. Leave the oven on.
Grind nuts in a food processor until quite fine. Remove 2 tablespoons; set aside. Add flour, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon, lemon zest, and salt to the processor bowl; process to blend. With the motor running, gradually add butter through the feed tube; process just until blended.
Whisk eggs with vanilla in a small bowl. Gradually add egg mixture to the processor, pulsing on and off just until the mixture forms dough (do not over-process, or the dough will be too sticky). Divide dough into two portions.
Working between two sheets of waxed paper, roll out one portion of dough to an 11-inch circle. Slide the dough onto a baking sheet; freeze for 15 minutes.
Press the remaining dough into the bottom of a lightly buttered 9-inch square pan. Refrigerate until the top crust is ready.
Spread the chilled bottom crust with raspberry jam. Remove the baking sheet from the freezer. Peel off the top layer of waxed paper. Cut the dough into a perfect 9-inch square using a ruler or the pan as a guide. Invert the top crust onto the jam-spread bottom crust in the pan. Remove any remaining sheet of waxed paper. Lightly sprinkle the crust with the reserved hazelnuts. Bake until the top is golden brown, approximately 25 to 30 minutes. Let cool on a wire rack.
Using a small strainer, sprinkle evenly with powdered sugar. Let stand at least 3 hours before cutting into bars. Yield: 18 bars.
